title: Ansible AWX Job Template Concurrent Execution Queue Depth
dataSources: `index=iac` `sourcetype=ansible:awx` metrics snapshot from Automation Controller `/api/v2/metrics/` or scheduled job inventory API
app: AWX / Ansible Automation Platform metrics exporter to Splunk (scripted input)
spl:
index=iac sourcetype="ansible:awx"
| eval pending=coalesce(pending_jobs, queue_pending, jobs_pending)
| eval running=coalesce(running_jobs, jobs_running)
| where isnotnull(pending)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(pending) as max_pending, max(running) as max_running, latest(instance_group) as ig by _time, controller_host
| where max_pending > 25
| sort -max_pending
description: AWX serializes work across instance groups; a growing pending queue means execution capacity, database performance, or container limits are saturated. Early detection preserves SLA for patching and compliance runs that must finish overnight.
value: Avoids surprise job backlog during patch Tuesday and informs horizontal scaling of execution planes.
implementation: Poll `/api/v2/metrics/` or custom telemetry job that emits pending_jobs and running_jobs per controller. Tag `instance_group`. Correlate with PostgreSQL latency and container CPU on execution nodes.
visualization: Area chart (pending vs running over time), single-value gauge (max pending), table by instance group.

references.title: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform — Metrics

grandmaExplanation: We watch queueing and wait time so developers are not stuck behind broken runners or clogged pipelines.
knownFalsePositives: Build times vary with cache state, runner availability, or test suite expansion.
